Name: SMTP too long line
description: Some antivirus scanners dies when they process an email with a too long string without line breaks. 2 RESULTS PER HOST 3 Such a message was sent. If there is an antivirus on your MTA, it might have crashed. Please check its status right now, as it is not possible to do it remotely.
solution: Solution type: VendorFix
Contact the vendor of the antivirus scanner to get an update.
detection_result: Vulnerability was detected according to the Vulnerability Detection Method.

Name: OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager Default Credentials
description: The remote OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager is installed/configured in a way that it has account(s) with default passwords enabled.
solution: Solution type: Workaround
Change the password of the mentioned account(s).
impact: This issue may be exploited by a remote attacker to gain access to sensitive information or modify system configuration.
insight: It was possible to login with default credentials: admin/admin, sadmin/changeme, observer/observer or admin/openvas.
detection_result: It was possible to login using the following credentials (username:password:role
detection_method: Try to login with default credentials via the OMP/GMP protocol.
Details: OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager Default Credentials
product_detection_result: Detected by OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.103825)
Method: OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager Detection

Name: SSL/TLS: Certificate Expired
description: The remote server's SSL/TLS certificate has already expired.
solution: Solution type: Mitigation
Replace the SSL/TLS certificate by a new one.
insight: This script checks expiry dates of certificates associated with SSL/TLS-enabled services on the target and reports whether any have already expired.
detection_result: The certificate of the remote service expired on 2020-08-20 19:18:24.
issued by .: C=DE,L=Osnabrueck,O=OpenVAS Users,OU=Certificate Authority for 218ffb30ff7a

Name: Check if Mailserver answer to VRFY and EXPN requests
description: The Mailserver on this host answers to VRFY and/or EXPN requests.
solution: Solution type: Workaround
Disable VRFY and/or EXPN on your Mailserver.
For postfix add 'disable_vrfy_command=yes' in 'main.cf'.
For Sendmail add the option 'O PrivacyOptions=goaway'.
It is suggested that, if you really want to publish this type of information, you use a mechanism that legitimate users actually know about, such as Finger or HTTP.
insight: VRFY and EXPN ask the server for information about an address. They are inherently unusable through firewalls, gateways, mail exchangers for part-time hosts, etc.
detection_result: 'VRFY root' produces the following answer: 252 2.0.0 root
detection_method: Details: Check if Mailserver answer to VRFY and EXPN requests

- No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)

- The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and 'Add
historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the 'Global variable settings'
of the scan config in use
If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community portal.

Name: wapiti (NASL wrapper)
description: This plugin uses wapiti to find web security issues. Make sure to have wapiti 2.x as wapiti 1.x is not supported. See the preferences section for wapiti options. Note that the scanner is using limited set of wapiti options. Therefore, for more complete web assessment, you should use standalone wapiti tool for deeper/customized checks. Note: The plugin needs the 'wapiti' binary found within the PATH of the user running the scanner and needs to be executable for this user. The existence of this binary is checked and reported separately within 'Availability of scanner helper tools' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.810000).
detection_result: The wapiti report filename is empty. That could mean that a wrong version of wapiti is used or tmp dir is not accessible. Make sure to have wapiti 2.x as wapiti 1.x is not supported. In short: Check the installation of wapiti and the scanner.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several NVTs and tries to find the best matching OS. Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection. If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community portal.

Name: Traceroute
description: A traceroute from the scanning server to the target system was conducted. This traceroute is provided primarily for informational value only. In the vast majority of cases, it does not represent a vulnerability. However, if the displayed traceroute contains any private addresses that should not have been publicly visible, then you have an issue you need to correct.
solution: Block unwanted packets from escaping your network.
detection_result: Here is the route from 127.0.0.1 to 127.0.0.1:
